questions.q: By which famous epithet is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel remembered?
questions.explanation: Patel is remembered as the Iron Man of India for his firm leadership and the integration of the princely states.

questions.q: Sardar Patel earned the title 'Sardar' after leading which satyagraha in 1928?
questions.explanation: Patel earned the title Sardar, meaning leader, after successfully leading the Bardoli Satyagraha of 1928.

questions.q: Patel served as independent India's first holder of which two offices?
options: Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister
questions.explanation: Patel became independent India's first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister.

questions.q: What was Patel's most celebrated achievement after independence?
options: Integrating the princely states into the Union
questions.explanation: Patel is best known for integrating the hundreds of princely states into the Indian Union.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Sardar Patel born?
questions.explanation: Patel was born in Gujarat and is closely associated with the Gujarat region of the freedom movement.

questions.q: What was Sardar Patel's profession before he joined the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: Patel trained as a barrister and practised law before devoting himself to the national movement.

questions.q: Sardar Patel was a leading organizer of which political party?
questions.explanation: Patel was a leading organizer of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: The towering 'Statue of Unity', unveiled in 2018, honours which freedom fighter?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity in Gujarat is a memorial to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.

questions.q: Patel worked closely under the inspiration of which leader during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Patel was a devoted follower and close lieutenant of Mahatma Gandhi in the Congress.

questions.q: The Bardoli Satyagraha that Patel led was a protest by which group against the British?
options: Peasants against a steep land-revenue hike
questions.explanation: The Bardoli Satyagraha of 1928 was a peasant movement against a sharp increase in land revenue, which Patel led to success.

questions.q: In which district of Gujarat did the 1928 Bardoli Satyagraha take place?
questions.explanation: Bardoli is a taluka in the Surat district of Gujarat, where Patel led the famous 1928 satyagraha.

questions.q: Before Bardoli, Patel had also led peasants in which earlier 1918 satyagraha in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Patel played a leading role in the Kheda Satyagraha of 1918, an early peasant campaign in Gujarat.

questions.q: Which senior official worked closely with Patel as Secretary of the States Ministry to integrate the princely states?
questions.explanation: V. P. Menon worked closely with Patel as the key civil servant in integrating the princely states.

questions.q: Patel was elected president of the Indian National Congress in which year?
questions.explanation: Patel presided over the Karachi session of the Congress as its president in 1931.

questions.q: Most princely states were integrated through an 'Instrument of Accession'. Which large state's accession became a particularly difficult problem for Patel?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad's reluctant Nizam posed one of the hardest integration problems, ultimately resolved by police action in 1948.

questions.q: The 1948 'Operation Polo' police action that integrated Hyderabad was carried out under whose firm direction as Home Minister?
questions.explanation: The police action against Hyderabad in 1948 was directed by Patel as Home Minister.

questions.q: What was Sardar Patel's full name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Vallabhbhai Jhaverbhai Patel.

questions.q: Patel's elder brother, also a notable politician and Congress figure, was?
questions.explanation: Patel's elder brother Vithalbhai Patel was a prominent politician who served as President of the Central Legislative Assembly.

questions.q: In which year did Sardar Patel die?
questions.explanation: Patel died in December 1950, a few years after independence.

questions.q: During the Quit India Movement of 1942, what happened to Patel?
options: He was arrested and imprisoned along with other Congress leaders
questions.explanation: Patel, like other senior Congress leaders, was arrested and imprisoned during the Quit India Movement of 1942.

questions.q: Patel is regarded as the patron and unifier of which all-India administrative service?
questions.explanation: Patel championed a strong all-India civil service and is regarded as the patron saint of the Indian Administrative Service.

questions.q: Patel's birthday, 31 October, is observed in India as which national day?
questions.explanation: Patel's birth anniversary on 31 October is marked as National Unity Day in recognition of his role in unifying India.

questions.q: During the 1928 Bardoli Satyagraha, what tactic did the peasants principally adopt under Patel's guidance?
options: Refusal to pay the enhanced land revenue
questions.explanation: The Bardoli peasants, led by Patel, refused to pay the enhanced land revenue, forcing the government to roll back the increase.

questions.q: In the 1946 contest for Congress presidency, who withdrew so that the eventual choice fell on Nehru rather than himself?
questions.explanation: Patel had the support of several provincial committees in 1946 but withdrew at Gandhi's wish, clearing the way for Nehru.

questions.q: Which small Gujarat princely state's ruler tried to accede to Pakistan despite a Hindu-majority population, a crisis Patel firmly resolved in India's favour?
questions.explanation: The ruler of Junagadh sought to accede to Pakistan, but Patel ensured its integration with India after a plebiscite favoured India.

questions.q: Patel served as chairman of which important committee of the Constituent Assembly?
options: The Committee on Fundamental Rights and Minorities
questions.explanation: Patel chaired the Advisory Committee on Fundamental Rights and Minorities in the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: After Bardoli's success, the Government appointed an inquiry committee whose report largely vindicated the peasants' grievances. What did it recommend?
options: A sharp reduction in the revenue assessment
questions.explanation: The Maxwell-Broomfield inquiry following the Bardoli agitation found the revenue increase unjustified and recommended a sharp reduction.

questions.q: Patel led the relief and rehabilitation effort for refugees from which event in 1947?
questions.explanation: As Home Minister, Patel oversaw the massive task of resettling refugees displaced by the Partition of India in 1947.

questions.q: In which Gujarat city did Patel begin his public life, serving for a time as its municipal president?
questions.explanation: Patel entered public life in Ahmedabad, where he served on and later led the municipality before joining the national movement.

questions.q: Patel was posthumously awarded India's highest civilian honour in 1991. Which award was it?
questions.explanation: Patel was posthumously conferred the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award, in 1991.
